description_long: This object is a pair of scissors with an unusual, elongated design, featuring two parallel handles and two long, sharply pointed blades. The handles are rectangular and appear to be made of a metallic material with a slightly weathered, bronze-like finish. The blades are dark, metallic, and taper to very fine points, suggesting they are designed for precision cutting or piercing. The central joint connecting the blades is minimalistic, with no visible screw or rivet, giving the scissors a sleek, almost futuristic appearance. There are no finger loops; instead, the handles are straight bars, which may require a different grip technique compared to traditional scissors. The overall construction appears robust, with a focus on durability and sharpness.
description: A pair of elongated, metallic scissors with parallel bar handles and sharply pointed blades, featuring a minimalistic central joint and a weathered bronze finish.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the scissors from an angled top perspective, highlighting the parallel bar handles and the sharp, dark blades.
description_view_1: View 1 presents the scissors from the opposite angle, emphasizing the length of the blades and the straight, rectangular handles.
description_view_2: View 2 offers a clear look at the pointed blade tips and the symmetrical alignment of the handles.
description_view_3: View 3 displays the scissors from a side angle, showcasing the thickness of the handles and the sleekness of the blade joint.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, rectangular, symmetrical handles with no ergonomic or padded features; ambidextrous design.
attribute_blade_joint_type: Minimalistic, non-visible joint; likely a concealed rivet or welded connection with no adjustability.
attribute_finger_loop_design: No finger loops; handles are straight bars, requiring a pinch or grip hold rather than traditional finger insertion.
attribute_blade_tip_style: Sharply pointed blade tips designed for precision cutting or piercing.
